parsed_findings.chain_steps: Bypass ownership check -> mass assignment -> privilege escalation

parsed_findings.vuln_claim: IDOR: auth check only blocks if BOTH id mismatch AND not support; any authenticated user can modify other users

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Authenticate as user A with balance 100. 2) Fire two concurrent POST /transfer {to:B,amount:100}. 3) Both read src=100, both pass check, both write 0; recipient gets 200.
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can transfer more funds than their balance, effectively creating money or zeroing other accounts.
parsed_findings.proof: Two concurrent requests both read src before either write completes, both pass the balance check, and both deduct from the same account.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send two simultaneous POST /transfer requests for the full balance; both will pass line 6 and deduct, draining more than available.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Race condition (TOCTOU) in balance check-then-update allows double-spend

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) POST /transfer with amount=-1000. 2) Line 6: src(0) < -1000 is False, passes. 3) Line 8 sets attacker balance to 0-(-1000)=1000.
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can set their own balance arbitrarily high by transferring a negative amount to themselves.
parsed_findings.proof: int('-999') succeeds; src < amt is False for negative amt, so attacker transfers -999 from victim to self, increasing their own balance.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /transfer {to:attacker_id, amount:-500}; src(attacker)=0 < -500 is False, so attacker balance becomes 0-(-500)=500, victim gains nothing deducted.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: No validation that amount is positive allows negative transfers to steal funds

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Send to field with SQL metacharacters. 2) db.get_balance interpolates into query. 3) Injected clause affects unintended rows.
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker could corrupt balances of arbitrary users or extract data depending on the ORM/query construction.
parsed_findings.proof: to=request.json['to'] is used directly in db.get_balance(to) and db.set_balance(to,...) with no sanitization or type enforcement.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /transfer {to:"1 OR 1=1--", amount:1}; if db methods use string interpolation, this manipulates the query affecting unintended rows.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: 'to' field passed unsanitized to db calls may enable SQL injection if db methods interpolate input

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Send malformed/self-signed JWT -> verify() throws -> NODE_ENV==production -> decode() returns attacker-controlled claims as auth context
parsed_findings.impact: Full authentication bypass in production — attacker can impersonate any user or escalate privileges.

parsed_findings.repro_steps: Forge a JWT with arbitrary claims, send with invalid signature; in NODE_ENV=production the catch block returns decoded payload as trusted identity.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Failed signature verification silently falls back to unverified jwt.decode() in production.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Exploit timing oracle to recover want value, then send forged request with matching signature to execute arbitrary process_event payloads.
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can forge valid HMAC signatures without knowing SECRET, enabling arbitrary webhook injection.
parsed_findings.proof: got == want uses Python's built-in == which short-circuits on first differing byte, leaking timing info about the correct signature.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send many requests with signatures differing at each byte position; measure response times to recover the expected HMAC byte-by-byte.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Timing side-channel in HMAC comparison via non-constant-time string equality

parsed_findings.chain_steps: overflow buf[64] → overwrite saved RIP → redirect to shellcode or ROP chain
parsed_findings.impact: Arbitrary code execution / RCE with attacker-controlled return address.
parsed_findings.proof: gets() has no length limit; input >64 bytes overwrites return address on the stack.

parsed_findings.vuln_claim: gets() writes unbounded input into a 64-byte stack buffer, enabling stack-based buffer overflow.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Inverted guard → NULL out accepted → memcpy(NULL,in,n) → crash/memory corruption
parsed_findings.impact: Write to NULL pointer causes immediate crash (DoS); on some embedded targets could overwrite low memory leading to code execution.
parsed_findings.proof: if (out || out_sz == 0) return -1; — should be `!out`, so NULL out is accepted and dereferenced at line 5-6.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Call copy_name("x", NULL, 10); guard passes (NULL is falsy but OR means out_sz==0 is false so whole expr is false… wait: NULL||10==0 = 0||0 = 0 → doesn't return; proceeds to memcpy(NULL,...)).
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Null pointer dereference guard is inverted; condition `out || out_sz==0` passes when out is NULL, allowing memcpy to write to NULL.
